name: Redfin Off-Market Estimate Checker
tagline: Find out if you can trust Redfin's estimate on a property that isn't for sale.
problem: Homeowners and investors look at Redfin's off-market estimate to gauge what a property is worth — but there's no easy way to know if that number is reliable for a specific neighborhood.
solution: I built a tool that audits Redfin's prediction accuracy by comparing its estimates on nearby recently-sold homes to the actual sale prices.
result: You get a concrete accuracy report for the neighborhood, so you know whether to trust the number or adjust your expectations.
description: Redfin shows an estimate on every property — even ones that aren't on the market. But how accurate is that number? This tool checks Redfin's track record on nearby homes that actually sold, comparing what Redfin predicted before each sale to the real sale price. You get a neighborhood accuracy report so you know whether to trust the estimate on the property you're looking at.
whoFor: Homeowners checking their own property value, investors scoping off-market opportunities, or anyone evaluating a Redfin estimate on a property that isn't currently listed for sale.
useCommandHint: Paste a Redfin property URL into Claude, then say:
useCommand: How accurate is Redfin's estimate on this off-market property?
requirements: Claude desktop app with Cowork mode enabled, plus the Claude in Chrome extension installed and active in your Chrome browser.
